subject: CS
requirement_designation: Continuing Comm.& Writing with Race & Ethnicity(*)
requirements: Continuing Comm.& Writing with Race & Ethnicity(*)
course_notes: Course Description: This interdisciplinary seminar course functions to orient students to some of the key texts and concepts in queer theory and to explore some of the emerging scholarship that falls under the sign of queer. This later scholarship will engage critical approaches to race, ethnicity and indigeneity, geopolitics, science and technology as well as analyses of humanity and its correlates. Throughout we will consider the kinds of theoretical work that queer can (and cannot) perform and the ways that queerness may have bearing on the formation of theory.
